body:
This PR is an alternative to #1340.

One thing I found from this is that we are currently running `test/Spaces/spaces.jl`, and one other test, twice.

Iterating over all the tests has a few nice advantages:
 - We can ensure we're not doing duplicate work
 - We can tabulate the results, to make it easier to see which tests are expensive
 - We could search for all tests in `test/` starting with `unit_` to see if we're missing any

Another thing I did was, I added a flag: `prevent_leaky_tests`, which allows us to toggle on and off (effectively) using `@safetestset`. We could even make this `true` on GHA, and `false` locally, so that running unit tests locally is much faster, but CI could still catch leaky test situations (which will happen anyway on buildkite).

I think this might be a bit simpler/declarative than #1340, so I'm inclined to favor this PR over #1340. Thoughts?
title: Iterate over unit tests, tabulate sorted timings
